MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA) as Successor Administrator; William Klein for Timothy Cook (Former Administrator) and with James J. McHale, CPA; Casimir Wilson as former Attorney for Christopher Cook; Christopher Cook in Propria Persona; Richard Wise for and with Creditor Dean Littlewood; Dennis Vann special appearance for Amy Harrington and with Timothy Cook (Beneficiary).
The Court inquired of Mr. Kline's Interim Accounting pursuant to court order that was submitted; the Attachments in the Inventory and Appraisal, DWS Scudder Account listed in the amount of $79,269.00 not an asset of the probate estate. In open court Mr. McHale presented a copy of the most current, un-served but verified supplemental Accounting of the two Scudder Accounts and American Century Investment Account. Further, he stated his concern with the filing of the tax return and need to obtain an IRS transcript to complete the accounting. Mr. Ferdon agreed to file a Petition to amend to correct the Inventory and Appraisal for any objection to be made by the parties if needed. Mr. Casimir stated that both side are willing to stipulate and are ready for distributions. Counsel discussed with the court of the funds in Mr. Klein's trust account and the blocked account and the funds to pay taxes, creditor, attorney fees and costs. Without opposition, the court granted the accounting fees of $12,680.00 to Mr. McHale to be taken from Mr. Kline's trust account and appointed Mr. McHale to prepare the Owl Street bar's accounting and tax return as needed. The Court stated to parties to further discussions and mediations necessary to resolved all issues with a global settlement, and to preserve the resources of the estate. Counsel agreed to continued matters to Mar. 18, 2009 for filing of the Amended Petition by SFPA. Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Sajja Ravinantapricha.
